exercises.title: Minimal custom exception
exercises.scenario: Build the skeleton of a custom exception class.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete a minimal `ProductNotFoundException`.

blanks.hint: Parameter name for the error message

hints: Unchecked: extends RuntimeException
Constructor takes a String message
super(message) passes message to RuntimeException

exercises.title: Trace custom exception message
exercises.scenario: getMessage() returns what was passed to super().
hints: super(message) in constructor sets the message
e.getMessage() returns what was passed to super()
Message is built in the constructor: 'Age 16 below required 18'

exercises.title: Create and throw a custom exception
exercises.scenario: Define, throw, and catch your own exception.
exercises.instructions:
Create `ScoreOutOfRangeException extends RuntimeException`. Throw it if score is not 0-100. Catch it in main.

Expected output:
Score 75 is valid
ScoreOutOfRangeException: Score 150 is out of range (0-100)
exercises.starter_code: // Define ScoreOutOfRangeException, throw for invalid scores

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Score 75 is valid
ScoreOutOfRangeException: Score 150 is out of range (0-100)

hints: class ScoreOutOfRangeException extends RuntimeException
Constructor takes int score, calls super('Score ' + score + ' is out of range (0-100)')
throw new ScoreOutOfRangeException(score) when out of range

exercises.title: Custom exception with domain fields
exercises.scenario: Carry structured data in the exception.
exercises.instructions:
Create `InsufficientStockException` with fields: `productName` and `requested` and `available`. Message: 'productName: requested N but only M available'.

Expected output:
InsufficientStockException: Keyboard: requested 5 but only 2 available
Product name: Keyboard
Requested: 5, Available: 2
exercises.starter_code: // Custom exception with productName, requested, available fields

test_cases.expected_stdout:
InsufficientStockException: Keyboard: requested 5 but only 2 available
Product name: Keyboard
Requested: 5, Available: 2

hints: Three final fields: productName, requested, available
super(productName + ': requested ' + requested + ' but only ' + available + ' available')
Getters for each field

exercises.title: Two-constructor pattern
exercises.instructions:
Create `DatabaseException extends RuntimeException` with BOTH constructors. Show the wrapping constructor being used.

Expected output:
Direct: Connection pool exhausted
Wrapped: Query failed: original: timeout after 3000ms

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Direct: Connection pool exhausted
Wrapped: Query failed: original: timeout after 3000ms

hints: Constructor 1: (String message) calls super(message)
Constructor 2: (String message, Throwable cause) calls super(message, cause)
e.getCause() retrieves the wrapped exception

exercises.title: Checked custom exception with throws
exercises.scenario: Custom checked exception in method signature.
exercises.instructions:
Create `FileParseException extends Exception` (checked). Use it in `parseConfig(String content)` which throws it if content is empty. Declare in method signature.

Expected output:
Config: debug=true, timeout=30
FileParseException: Config content is empty

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Config: debug=true, timeout=30
FileParseException: Config content is empty

hints: FileParseException extends Exception — checked
Method declares throws FileParseException
Caller must catch (or re-declare)

exercises.title: Exception hierarchy — base + two subclasses
exercises.scenario: Group related failures under a base exception.
exercises.instructions:
Create:
- `LoginException extends RuntimeException` (base)
- `AccountLockedException extends LoginException` — includes lockDuration (minutes)
- `InvalidCredentialsException extends LoginException` — includes attemptNumber

Handle each differently in main.

Expected output:
Locked! Account locked for 30 minutes
Wrong password (attempt 2 of 3)
exercises.starter_code: // Login exception hierarchy: base + 2 specific subclasses

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Locked! Account locked for 30 minutes
Wrong password (attempt 2 of 3)

hints: AccountLockedException and InvalidCredentialsException both extend LoginException
Catch specific subclasses first
Each subclass carries different domain data

exercises.title: OrderException with item details
exercises.scenario: Rich domain exception for an e-commerce order.
exercises.instructions:
Create `OrderException extends RuntimeException` with:
- `orderId` (String)
- `reason` (String)
- `failedItem` (String, may be null)

Message: 'Order #orderId failed: reason [item: failedItem]' (skip item part if null).

Throw it for: out-of-stock item, payment declined.

Expected output:
Order #ORD-001 failed: Item 'Headphones' is out of stock [item: Headphones]
Order #ORD-002 failed: Payment declined by bank
exercises.starter_code: // OrderException with orderId, reason, failedItem fields

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Order #ORD-001 failed: Item 'Headphones' is out of stock [item: Headphones]
Order #ORD-002 failed: Payment declined by bank

hints: Build message in a static helper method called from super()
Null check on failedItem for optional message part
Three final fields with getters

exercises.title: Wrap third-party exception in custom
exercises.scenario: Convert library exception into domain exception.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate wrapping: a data parsing library throws `java.text.ParseException` (checked). Wrap it in unchecked `DataImportException` that also carries the input string that failed.

Expected output:
Imported: ₹5000.0 on 2024-01-15
DataImportException: Failed to import '2024-99-99' — caused by: Unparseable date: "2024-99-99"
exercises.starter_code:
import java.text.*;

// DataImportException wraps ParseException, adds failedInput field

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Imported: ₹5000.0 on 2024-01-15
DataImportException: Failed to import '2024-99-99' — caused by: Unparseable date: "2024-99-99"

hints: DataImportException extends RuntimeException — unchecked
Constructor takes failedInput + cause: super('Failed to import ...' , cause)
Catch ParseException (checked), throw DataImportException (unchecked)

exercises.title: Hierarchy catch order with custom exceptions
exercises.scenario: Specific subclass caught before base class.
exercises.expected_solution:
NOT_FOUND: item gone
APP: general failure

test_cases.expected:
NOT_FOUND: item gone
APP: general failure

hints: NotFoundException extends AppException
Specific catch (NotFoundException) first — catches the subtype
Base catch (AppException) second — catches any other AppException

exercises.title: Full inventory exception system
exercises.scenario: Complete exception design for an inventory domain.
exercises.instructions:
Build an inventory exception system:
- `InventoryException extends RuntimeException` — base
- `ProductNotFoundException extends InventoryException` — productId field
- `InsufficientQuantityException extends InventoryException` — productId, requested, available

Inventory.remove(productId, qty) throws the right exception:

Expected output:
Removed 3 x Mouse (32 left)
ProductNotFoundException: Product 'TABLET-01' not found
InsufficientQuantityException: Mouse: requested 50, only 35 available
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Inventory exception hierarchy and Inventory class

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Removed 3 x Mouse (32 left)
ProductNotFoundException: Product 'TABLET-01' not found
InsufficientQuantityException: Mouse: requested 50, only 35 available

hints: After removing 3 Mice, 35-3=32 remain
Second test: TABLET-01 not in stock map → ProductNotFoundException
Third test: Mouse has 32, need 50 → InsufficientQuantityException

exercises.title: Exception message quality
exercises.scenario: Good vs bad exception messages — context matters.
exercises.instructions:
Same exception thrown with different message quality. Create `ValidationException`. Demonstrate: bad message (vague), good message (with entity ID and constraint).

Expected output:
Bad: invalid
Good: User ID-42: email 'notAnEmail' is not valid (must contain @)

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Bad: invalid
Good: User ID-42: email 'notAnEmail' is not valid (must contain @)

hints: Both throw ValidationException — difference is only the message
Good message: entity ID + field name + actual value + constraint
Shows why context in messages matters for debugging

exercises.title: E-commerce order exception hierarchy
exercises.scenario: Design a complete exception hierarchy for an order domain.
exercises.instructions:
Design exception hierarchy:
```
OrderException extends RuntimeException (base)
├── CartException (items issues)
│   └── OutOfStockException — itemId, requested, available
└── CheckoutException (checkout failures)
    ├── PaymentDeclinedException — cardLast4, declineReason
    └── AddressUnserviceableException — pincode
```

Test all four exception types.

Expected output:
OutOfStockException: Item 'MOUSE-01' requested 3 but only 1 in stock
PaymentDeclinedException: Card ending 4242 declined: insufficient_funds
AddressUnserviceableException: Pincode 999999 is not serviceable
Base CartException: Cart validation failed

test_cases.expected_stdout:
OutOfStockException: Item 'MOUSE-01' requested 3 but only 1 in stock
PaymentDeclinedException: Card ending 4242 declined: insufficient_funds
AddressUnserviceableException: Pincode 999999 is not serviceable
Base CartException: Cart validation failed

hints: Two branches: CartException and CheckoutException under OrderException
Each leaf has domain-specific fields
instanceof pattern matching to identify which subtype
CartException (base) catches only when no specific type matched

exercises.title: Custom exception in REST API response simulation
exercises.instructions:
Build an `ApiException` hierarchy where each exception maps to an HTTP status code.

- `ApiException extends RuntimeException` — base, with `httpStatus` field
- `NotFoundException extends ApiException` — status 404, resourceId field
- `UnauthorizedException extends ApiException` — status 401
- `ValidationException extends ApiException` — status 400, fieldErrors List<String>

`ApiController.process(String action)` throws appropriate exception per action. A `handleRequest` method returns a formatted response string.

Expected output:
200: OK — User profile loaded
404: NOT FOUND — Resource 'user-999' not found
401: UNAUTHORIZED — Authentication required
400: BAD REQUEST — Field errors: [name is required, email is invalid]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// ApiException hierarchy with HTTP status codes

test_cases.expected_stdout:
200: OK — User profile loaded
404: NOT FOUND — Resource 'user-999' not found
401: UNAUTHORIZED — Authentication required
400: BAD REQUEST — Field errors: [name is required, email is invalid]

hints: ApiException has httpStatus field — passed to super(status, msg)
Each subclass sets its own status code via super(statusCode, message)
ValidationException carries a List<String> of errors
handleRequest catches specific types to get structured data

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay-style payment exception system
exercises.scenario: Production-grade exception design for a payment service.
exercises.instructions:
Design the exception system for a payment processor:

Unchecked hierarchy:
```
RazorpayException (base, has: errorCode, description)
├── PaymentException (has: paymentId)
│   ├── CardDeclinedException (has: declineCode: insufficient_funds/stolen_card/expired_card)
│   └── PaymentGatewayException (has: gatewayId)
└── RefundException (has: refundId, reason)
```

Process 4 transactions and handle each specifically.

Expected output:
PAY-001 success: ₹5000.0
PAY-002 DECLINED (insufficient_funds): Ask user to add funds or try another card
PAY-003 GATEWAY ERROR (HDFC-GW): Retry in 30s or contact support
REFUND-001 FAILED: Refund deadline exceeded

test_cases.expected_stdout:
PAY-001 success: ₹5000.0
PAY-002 DECLINED (insufficient_funds): Ask user to add funds or try another card
PAY-003 GATEWAY ERROR (HDFC-GW): Retry in 30s or contact support
REFUND-001 FAILED: Refund deadline exceeded

hints: RazorpayException base carries errorCode and description
PaymentException extends RazorpayException — adds paymentId
CardDeclinedException and PaymentGatewayException extend PaymentException
RefundException is separate branch under RazorpayException
Each catch block uses the specific type's getter methods
